reasoning: The Lean signature takes `hsumm : Summable fun n => (1 - ‖zeros n‖)` as a hypothesis — but this is exactly the conclusion the informal statement asks us to prove. The Lean conclusion (existence of a limit for the partial sums) then follows trivially from `Summable`, independently of the holomorphy/boundedness hypotheses. Thus the Lean statement is essentially tautological and does not assert the Blaschke-type summability result the informal statement demands. The informal claim implies the Lean claim (summability ⇒ partial-sum convergence), but the Lean claim does not imply the informal one, so the formalization is strictly weaker.
